statement: Through the EEA Agreement, Iceland has already incorporated a substantial body of EU energy legislation, including the Third Energy Package (Directive 2009/72/EC on electricity and 2009/73/EC on gas), the Renewable Energy Directive (2009/28/EC and its 2018 recast), and energy efficiency directives. However, Iceland secured key adaptations: it is exempt from the EU's gas market rules (having no gas infrastructure) and has adaptations recognising its unique energy mix. The EEA Joint Committee Decision No 93/2017 incorporated the Third Energy Package with Icelandic adaptations. Iceland has not incorporated the EU Emissions Trading System into the EEA Agreement, instead maintaining a separate arrangement.
source_name: EFTA Secretariat — EEA Agreement Annex IV (Energy); Althingi EU Assessment Committee
caveats: The EEA Agreement's 'two-pillar' enforcement system means EFTA Surveillance Authority (ESA) rather than the European Commission oversees compliance. EEA incorporation often lags behind EU adoption — there is currently a backlog of unincorporated energy acts. Full EU membership would remove the ability to negotiate adaptations.

related_entries.statement: As of 2025, approximately 70% of EU internal market legislation has been incorporated into Icelandic law through the EEA Agreement. This covers free movement of goods, services, capital, and persons, but excludes fisheries, agriculture, and justice/home affairs.

Iceland participates in the EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) through a bilateral agreement with the EU (Decision 2015/1814 and predecessors), not through the EEA Agreement. The arrangement covers Iceland's industrial emissions, primarily from aluminium smelting and ferrosilicon production. In 2023, Iceland's EU ETS-covered installations accounted for roughly 1.8 million tonnes CO₂-equivalent, with the three aluminium smelters as the dominant participants. EU ETS allowance prices rose from under 10 EUR/tonne in 2018 to approximately 60–80 EUR/tonne in 2023–2024, significantly increasing compliance costs for Icelandic industry.

citations.report_title: Ræða þarf alla kaflana á nýjan leik

claims.claim_text: Margir samningakaflar um efnahags- og markaðsmál voru auðveldir vegna þess að Ísland er þegar í EES-samningnum.

citations.report_title: Þrír hópar sem myndu hagnast mest á aðild Íslands að Evrópusambandinu

claims.claim_text: Íslensk fyrirtæki fylgja nú þegar stórum hluta þeirra reglna sem gilda á innri markaði Evrópu og því væri óverulegt sem bættist við með fullri aðild.

citations.report_title: „Við hefðum náð meiri árangri með því að vera innan Evrópusambandsins“

claims.claim_text: Ísland á aðild að ETS-viðskiptakerfinu í gegnum EES-samninginn.
Ísland er eyja og ekki tengt meginlandi Evrópu, sem skapar sérstöðu í samningaviðræðum um ETS-kerfið.
